commands.review_summary:
The PR filters `toolCall` blocks from OpenAI-completions streaming output when the final mapped stop reason is not `toolUse`, and adds three focused stream tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main appends `delta.tool_calls` even when `finish_reason` maps to `stop`, and the proposed mocked stream exercises that path. I did not independently run the live llama.cpp/Qwen scenario.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-provider proof and maintainer acceptance of the finish-reason-as-authoritative compatibility tradeoff before merge; there is no narrow automation repair to request.

target.title: fix(gateway): strip spurious tool call blocks when provider signals stop
target.body:
## Summary

Strip spurious tool call content blocks from OpenAI-compatible completions streaming responses when the provider signals `finish_reason: "stop"` (not `"tool_calls"`).

## Problem

When an OpenAI-compatible provider (e.g., llama.cpp serving Qwen3.6 with thinking enabled) emits structured `delta.tool_calls` parsed from the model's reasoning/thinking output but correctly reports `finish_reason: "stop"`, the gateway unconditionally builds `toolCall` content blocks and the agent loop executes them.

This is caused by an asymmetric stop-reason guard in `processOpenAICompletionsStream`: the existing guard downgrades `stopReason` from `"toolUse"` to `"stop"` when no tool call blocks are present (line 2679-2681), but the inverse guard — stripping tool call blocks when `stopReason` is not `"toolUse"` — was missing.

## Fix

Add the missing inverse guard after the existing one:

```typescript
if (hasToolCalls && output.stopReason !== "toolUse") {
  output.content = output.content.filter((block) => block.type !== "toolCall");
}
```

This ensures that when a provider does not signal tool-use intent via `finish_reason`, any tool call blocks in the response are treated as spurious and discarded.

## Tests

Added three test cases to `openai-transport-stream.test.ts`:
1. `delta.tool_calls` + `finish_reason: "stop"` → tool call blocks stripped ✅
2. `delta.tool_calls` + `finish_reason: "tool_calls"` → tool calls preserved (regression) ✅
3. No tool calls + `finish_reason: "stop"` → no change (regression) ✅

All 354 tests pass.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Tool call XML in LLM reasoning block is executed by gateway when provider signals `finish_reason: "stop"`
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `54d1b9c5b6`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0
- **Failing proof before fix**: The guard at line 2678-2681 only handles one direction (toolUse→stop when no blocks). No guard exists for the inverse (stop with blocks present). Source-verified at `openai-transport-stream.ts:2678-2681`.
- **Exact steps after patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: All 354 tests pass, including 3 new tests covering the spurious tool call scenario
- **Observed result**: Spurious tool call blocks are stripped when `stopReason !== "toolUse"`, preventing execution of tool calls the provider did not intend
- **What was not tested**: Live llama.cpp + Qwen3.6 end-to-end test (mock-based unit test only)

Fixes #85161
target.author: jerry-xin
